description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden shingled roof supported by two vertical wooden posts. The well features a wooden crank mechanism with a handle, attached to a horizontal spindle that winds a rope connected to a wooden bucket. The stonework is rugged and uneven, giving the well an old-fashioned, rustic appearance. The roof is pitched and covered with small, rectangular wooden shingles, providing shelter for the well opening. The well sits on a circular patch of grass, suggesting it is placed in an open area. The overall construction appears sturdy, with visible weathering on the wood and stone.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden shingled roof, crank mechanism, and bucket, set on a grassy patch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden roof, crank handle, and bucket clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden post, bucket, and the pitched roof's profile.
description_view_2: Rear view with the stone well and roof visible, crank mechanism partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden post and bucket from behind, with the roof's angle prominent.
attribute_well_cover_design: Wooden shingled pitched roof supported by posts; no lid or slab covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden crank and spindle mechanism with a rope and wooden bucket attached.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high forms the protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
